cases.prompt: set up a code of conduct
we need a support doc so questions stop landing in the issue tracker
should this project use a CLA or DCO?
write a README for the project
add a bug report form with required fields
require code owner reviews before merging to main
add topics so the repo is easier to find
write a PR description for this change
add the standard community files to this repo
expected_behavior: inspects the repo first (language, test/build commands, maintainer identity) and writes CONTRIBUTING.md with the repo's real commands, not generic boilerplate
uses Contributor Covenant for CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md and flags the contact-method placeholder for the maintainer to fill
SECURITY.md points to private reporting and notes the PVR link 404s until enabled on a public repo
adds SUPPORT.md that routes questions away from the issue tracker
verifies the result, e.g. via scripts/check_health_files.py or the community profile API
covers sensitive paths including .github/ and CI/workflow files, mapped to real maintainer handles
warns that CODEOWNERS is inert until a ruleset requires code-owner review, and points to repo protections for enforcement
places the file in a valid location (.github/, root, or docs/)

cases.prompt: solo hobby project - do I really need all these files?
expected_behavior: right-sizes: recommends LICENSE + README + CI first, defers CODE_OF_CONDUCT/GOVERNANCE until outside contributors exist
gives a staged adoption order rather than an all-or-nothing dump
does not shame the user or add files they declined
puts the full unmodified MIT text in LICENSE at the repo root with correct year and owner
notes GitHub's license detection needs the standard text at the root
mentions SPDX identifier usage in package manifests where applicable
